title: Multi-Step Ancient DNA Extraction Protocol For Bone And Teeth
author: Valeria Mattiangeli

steps_raw.instruction: In advance of extraction, prepare bone powder. Measure 0.09 mg to 0.120 mg of bone powder into a 2 mL Eppendorf tube

Prepare the extraction buffer where n is the number of sample and control tubes plus one (for pipetting error). In the example below 15 sample tubes and one extraction control tube are used (15 + 1 + 1 = 17) When preparing the extraction buffer and prior to the addition of proteinase K , subject the buffer to 00:30:00 (30 minutes) of UV light A B C D 1 Reagent x 1 (ul) x 17 (ul) x n (ul) 2 Tris-HCl, 1M 20 340 3 SDS (2% final) 17 289 4 EDTA, 0.5 940 15,980 5 *** UV Prior To The Addition Of Proteinase K *** 6 Proteinase K (50 U/mL) 13 221
To each tube add 990 µL of extraction buffer to each tube of bone powder
Cover each tube in parafilm and vortex until the bone pellet is completely in solution.
Incubate Overnight, (20-24h) using a thermomixer 700 rpm, 37 °C, .

Prepare a fresh batch of extraction buffer where n is the number of sample and control tubes plus one (for pipetting error). In the example below 15 sample tubes and one extraction control tube are used (15 + 1 + 1 = 17) When preparing the extraction buffer and prior to the addition of proteinase K , subject the buffer to 00:30:00 (30 minutes) of UV light A B C D 1 Reagent x 1 (ul) x 17 (ul) x n (ul) 2 Tris-HCl, 1M 20 340 3 SDS (2% final) 17 289 4 EDTA, 0.5 940 15,980 5 *** UV Prior To The Addition Of Proteinase K *** 6 Proteinase K (50 U/mL) 13 221 �
Remove each tube from incubation, then spin each sample tube using a bench centrifuge for 00:10:00 (10min) at 10000 rpm, °C, .
Transfer the supernatant to new labelled 1.5 mL tubes, parafilm and freeze. Note: DNA can be purified from this supernatant. Defrost and move onto "Stage 7: Purification"
Add 990 µL of extraction buffer to each sample tube containing the remaining bone pellet.
Cover each tube in parafilm and vortex until the bone pellet is completely in solution.
Incubate Overnight, (20-24h) using a thermomixer 700 rpm, 37 °C, .

Optional additional extraction: prepare a fresh batch of extraction buffer where n is the number of sample and control tubes plus one (for pipetting error). In the example below 15 sample tubes and one extraction control tube are used (15 + 1 + 1 = 17) When preparing the extraction buffer and prior to the addition of proteinase K , subject the buffer to 00:30:00 (30 minutes) of UV light A B C D 1 Reagent x 1 (ul) x 17 (ul) x n (ul) 2 Tris-HCl, 1M 20 340 3 SDS (2% final) 17 289 4 EDTA, 0.5 940 15,980 5 *** UV Prior To The Addition Of Proteinase K *** 6 Proteinase K (50 U/mL) 13 221 �

Remove each tube from incubation, then spin each sample tube using a bench centrifuge for 00:10:00 (10 min) at maximum speed 13300 rpm, °C, / 17000 x g, °C, . If a substantial amount of bone pellet remains, an additional extraction step can be performed, for a total of three overnight digestions.

Optional additional extraction: transfer the supernatant to new labelled 1.5 mL tubes, parafilm and freeze. Note: DNA can be purified from this supernatant. Defrost and move onto "Stage 7 - Purification"
To each tube add 990 µL of extraction buffer to each tube of bone powder.
Cover each tube in parafilm and vortex until the bone pellet is completely in solution.
Incubate Overnight, (20-24h) using a thermomixer 700 rpm, 37 °C, .

Optional: If a third overnight extraction was performed, remove each tube from incubation, then spin each sample tube using a bench centrifuge for 00:10:00 (10 min) at maximum speed 13300 rpm, °C, / 17000 x g, °C, .

Prepare labelled Amicon filter columns, 1 per sample. Add 3 mL of TrisEDTA (x1) to each Amicon filter and add the supernatant (~ 1 mL ) to the appriopriate column, taking care not to transfer bone powder . The remaining bone powder tubes and can frozen and re-extracted if needed.

Spin columns at 2500 rpm, °C, ( 1200 x g, °C, ) in a centrifuge with a swing bucket rotor until the supernatant + TrisEDTA is at the 250 µL mark . This is typically 6-10 minutes, but sometimes longer.
Remove the columns from the centrifuge. Add an additional 3 mL of TrisEDTA (x1) to the filters.

Spin at 2500 rpm, °C, ( 1200 x g, °C, ) in a centrifuge with a swing bucket rotor until the supernatant + TrisEDTA is at the 100 µL mark . Discard flow through and keep the remaining 100 µL in the filter.

Prepare a MinElute Qiagen for each sample with 500 µL of PB and add the remaining 100 µL from the Amicon filter. Spin the MinElute columns for 13300 rpm, °C, 1 minute ( 17000 x g, °C, ). Discard the flow-through.
Add 750 µL of PE buffer Qiagen Catalog #19065 to each MinElute Qiagen column. Spin the MinElute columns for 13300 rpm, °C, 1 minute ( 17000 x g, °C, ). Discard the flow-through.
Dry-spin the MinElute Qiagen columns for 13300 rpm, °C, 1 minute ( 17000 x g, °C, ).
Place each column in a fresh 1.5 mL labeled collection Eppendorf tube. These are the final tubes and will contain the purified DNA.

Using a pipette, add 50 µL of EBT (see material) to the filter of each spin column. Wait 00:05:00 (5 min), and spin down columns at maximum speed 13300 rpm, °C, / 17000 x g, °C, . Collection tube lids should be angled away from the direction of spin.
Sample tubes containing the purified DNA extract should be stored in a -20 °C freezer.
Heat EBT ( 50 µL x n , where n is the number of tubes plus one) to 65 °C using a thermomixer.
